name: Chronic kidney disease
description: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) — long-standing reduction in kidney function; all stages and causes.

en.name: Chronic kidney disease
en.shortDescription: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) — long-standing reduction in kidney function; all stages and causes.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a long-standing reduction in kidney function (low glomerular filtration rate and/or kidney damage) persisting over months. In intensive care it is a pre-existing comorbidity that influences fluid and drug handling, the risk of acute-on-chronic kidney injury, and prognosis. In the APACHE IV severity model, severe chronic renal failure / dialysis dependence is one of the chronic health items; this set is broader (see Scope).

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *46271022 / 709044004 — Chronic kidney disease* with all descendants included. It therefore captures **all stages** (stage 1 through stage 5 / end-stage renal disease) and **all causes** (hypertensive, diabetic, obstructive, etc.). It is the general "has CKD" variable, not restricted to the dialysis-dependent or end-stage form.

## Mapping Notes

- Map any documented stage or cause of CKD to this set.
- CKD caused by diabetes is included here **and** the diabetes diagnosis would map to the Diabetes mellitus set — the two are recorded independently.
- Acute kidney injury (a reversible, short-term event) is a distinct condition and is **not** part of this set.
